methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.1B - $8.0B.

data.narrative:
Fleet Space Technologies, a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $70M+, is valued through comparison to a narrow peer set of three same-stage private companies and a stage-progression framework. The broader comp universe includes five public space & aerospace tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private peers, though public comps cannot be applied here due to lack of revenue disclosure.

The stage-progression method, anchoring to $70M in total raised capital, applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $119M. The peer-median approach triangulates across three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Sierra Space ($8.0B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), and Skyryse ($1.1B)—producing a median implied value of $1.5B. The wide range ($1.1B to $8.0B) reflects material heterogeneity within the peer cohort, particularly Sierra Space's significant valuation premium. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 25.8x across the five tickers) remains inapplicable without disclosed revenue.

Triangulation across these two methods supports a valuation range of $101M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $810M. This broad dispersion reflects key uncertainties: whether Fleet's business model and scale trajectory align more closely with the lower-capitalized Skyryse or mid-market Varda, and the extent to which Sierra Space's $8B valuation reflects sector-wide investor appetite or company-specific factors. Stage and capital raised alone provide insufficient resolution to narrow this range meaningfully.
